sanskrit:
एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय।
अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा॥
transliteration:
etad-yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇīty-upadhāraya
ahaṃ kṛtsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas-tathā
literal_meaning: These two wombs give birth to all beings—understand this. I am the source of manifestation and the dissolution of the entire universe.
contextual_meaning: All inner and outer experiences—thoughts, feelings, body sensations, relationships—emerge from and dissolve back into a single conscious field. Identifying with that field rather than its contents ends the cycle of psychological birth-and-death.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic openness: the ego’s story has collapsed, leaving a vertiginous spaciousness that can either trigger panic or invite surrender.
trigger_scenario: You wake up from anesthesia or a deep meditation and, for a moment, can’t remember who you are; panic flares, then gives way to wordless presence.
anchor_text: Rest as the aware space in which every experience is born and dies.
